Financial Stress Index Signal
← Back to dashboard

Financial Stress Index Signal

Market-wide financial stress and systemic risk conditions.

As of 2026-02-07

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • The Financial Stress Index (1) indicates financial conditions are currently Neutral as of 2026-01-30. This represents a stabilisation after a period of elevated stress in late 2025.
  • Conviction Band: Medium | Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence | Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Key Dynamics:

  • The St. Louis Fed Financial Stress Index (STLFSI4) stands at -0.6784, below the zero threshold which typically signifies looser conditions (1).
  • The robust z-score for financial stress, `Stress_z`, is -0.242225, having receded from "Bearish" levels seen in November and December 2025.
  • This shift from positive z-scores (elevated stress) to a near-zero z-score implies easing relative to historical volatility.
  • Conditional Invalidation: A rise in the `Stress_z` above +0.5 would reverse the "Neutral" classification to "Bearish", indicating renewed financial stress.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Financial stress remains "Neutral" with the STLFSI4 level negative and the z-score between -0.5 and +0.5.
  • Upside secondary: A sustained drop in the `Stress_z` below -0.5, alongside the negative STLFSI4, would signal "Bullish" (risk-on) conditions.
  • Downside residual: A re-acceleration of financial stress, pushing the `Stress_z` above +0.5, would trigger a "Bearish" regime.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months). The signal monitors shifts in financial conditions that typically evolve over several months.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium. This signal provides a direct gauge of financial stability, crucial for broader macro composites.

Macro Relevance:

  • This signal primarily informs the liquidity and financial stress dimensions of the macro environment (1).
  • It suggests a mid-cycle position where financial conditions are not excessively tight or loose, supporting moderate growth.
  • Typically, easing financial stress complements stronger growth signals and can precede risk-on sentiment in equity markets.

Data & References:

  • St. Louis Fed Financial Stress Index (STLFSI4), latest observation 2026-01-30 (1).
  • The STLFSI4 value of -0.6784 and `Stress_z` of -0.242225 are most influential for the current state.
  • Additional public datasets like corporate credit spreads (e.g., ICE BofA spreads) or the VIX could enhance depth.
